items.title: Consumer Tech News (Feb 10-Feb 14): US & UK Denied Signing Global AI Safety Declaration, Musk To Settle $10 Million Trump Lawsuit & More
items.author: Lekha Gupta
items.description: The US and UK declined to sign a global AI safety declaration, Lyft reported strong Q4 earnings, Elon Musk agreed to settle a lawsuit with Donald Trump, and OpenAI's ChatGPT became the 6th most-visited website globally.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Apple rolled out iOS and iPadOS updates, ramped up recruitment for its home robotics division, and played a major role in the Super Bowl without overtly advertising its products.
Alphabet confirmed the date for its annual Google I/O developer conference.
Alphabet confirmed the date for its annual Google I/O developer conference.
Lyft reported strong Q4 revenue and net income results.
T-Mobile offered free access to its satellite-to-cell beta service powered by SpaceX's Starlink.

items.title: 3 Reasons to Buy Toast Stock Like There's No Tomorrow
items.author: Jennifer Saibil
items.description: Toast, a cloud-based AI company serving the restaurant industry, is seeing strong growth and profitability. With a large market opportunity, international expansion, and new product offerings, Toast stock is a compelling buy despite its recent stock gains.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Toast's strong growth, profitability, and large market opportunity, making it a compelling investment.
